title: Make sitemap.ts and robots.ts request-host-aware
description:
## Context

`src/app/sitemap.ts:7` and `src/app/robots.ts:44` hardcode `https://www.exponential.im`. Preview deploys and staging environments emit a sitemap that points back to production — which is a real SEO and QA bug today (search engines and ops dashboards see wrong URLs). Making them host-aware fixes that and aligns them with the rest of the URL centralization work.

## Scope

1. Update `src/app/sitemap.ts` to build absolute URLs from `headers().get('host')` (via `next/headers`) — or from `getPublicBaseUrl()` if exponential-x4z0 has already introduced it. Prefer the helper if available.
2. Same for `src/app/robots.ts`: the `Sitemap:` directive should reference the current host.
3. Handle the protocol correctly: `x-forwarded-proto` header if present, otherwise default to `https` in prod, `http` in dev.

## Files to modify

- `src/app/sitemap.ts`
- `src/app/robots.ts`

## Dependencies / coordination

- Soft dependency on exponential-x4z0 (URL helper). If that bead is done first, use `getPublicBaseUrl()` directly. If not, duplicate the logic locally — exponential-x4z0's implementer will collapse them later.

## Verification

- Visit `http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml` → all `<loc>` entries start with `http://localhost:3000`
- Visit `http://localhost:3000/robots.txt` → `Sitemap:` line points to `http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml`
- `curl -H 'Host: staging.example.com' -H 'X-Forwarded-Proto: https' http://localhost:3000/robots.txt` → reflects `https://staging.example.com`
- Deploy preview on Vercel: sitemap should reflect the preview URL, not production
- `npm run check` passes

## Notes for implementer

- `headers()` in app-router handlers is async in Next 15 — make sure you `await` it
- Don't break the signature Next.js expects (`export default function sitemap(): MetadataRoute.Sitemap`) — the function can still be async
- Keep the list of sitemap entries the same; only change how absolute URLs are built
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: Users invited to workspace don't get added when they sign up via magic link/OAuth (invitation not auto-accepted)
description:
When a user is invited to a workspace via workspace.addMember, a WorkspaceInvitation record is created and an email is sent with an /invite/{token} link. This link is only honored if the invitee clicks it, signs in, and clicks 'Accept Invitation' on the /invite/{token} page.

If the invitee signs up via any other route (direct sign-in, OAuth, or a magic link whose callbackUrl no longer points at /invite/{token}), their pending invitation is never accepted. They land in just their Personal workspace with no knowledge of the invite.

Fix: Added acceptPendingInvitationsForUser() helper in src/server/auth/config.ts that auto-accepts all pending (non-expired) workspace and team invitations matching the user's email. Called from both the createUser event (new sign-ups) and the signIn callback (existing users who received an invite after sign-up). For new users the first accepted workspace becomes their default workspace so they land there after sign-up instead of the empty Personal workspace.
notes: Scope expanded: (1) fixed auto-accept of pending invites via NextAuth createUser+signIn callbacks, (2) redesigned /invite/[token] logged-out state with dark two-pane auth surface (left form + right marketing preview), (3) brought same aesthetic to /signin. All hex literals moved to src/styles/globals.css under .auth-surface scope to satisfy pre-commit hook; feature stylesheet at src/styles/auth-surface.css references them via var() only. Real product logo (/expo-logo-20.png) used in both headers. SignInButtons.tsx removed (no longer used).

title: Stop duplicating SECURITY_POLICY in both chat/stream route and every agent's instructions
description:
SECURITY_POLICY (~148 lines) is injected twice per Anthropic call:

1. Server-side in src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts:290 — prepended as the highest-priority system message
2. Embedded inside each Mastra agent's static instructions:
   - mastra/src/mastra/agents/zoe-agent.ts (imports from ../agents/security-policy)
   - mastra/src/mastra/agents/assistant-agent.ts (imports from ../agents/security-policy)
   - mastra/src/mastra/agents/index.ts (Paddy/projectManager)

Both import from mastra/src/mastra/agents/security-policy.ts.

Pick ONE location. Recommended: keep it in the Mastra agent instructions only, and remove the server-side injection at stream/route.ts:290. Reasoning:
- Mastra agents are reused across multiple transports (chat/stream, Slack webhook, Telegram, WhatsApp, Signal). Only the stream route injects the policy server-side; the other transports already rely on the agent-embedded copy. Keeping the policy in the agent guarantees consistent security across all transports.
- The stream route's 'inject at top' pattern would matter only if the agent's own copy couldn't be trusted — but it's the same SECURITY_POLICY import either way.

Alternative: keep server-side, strip from agents — but then Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp lose the policy unless their entry points also inject it. More risky.

Acceptance:
- SECURITY_POLICY appears exactly once in the final prompt sent to Anthropic (verify via logging)
- All transports (stream, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Signal) still have the policy applied
- Saves ~1-2k tokens per request (~5% input cost)
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 1: Foundation — extract priority/projectColor/syncStatus/dates/types utilities, unify Priority type, promote design tokens
description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 1).

Goal: extract a shared 'actions list' layer composed by both `ActionList.tsx` and `TodayView.tsx` so the redesigned visual style works everywhere ActionList is used today, without losing any of ActionList's existing functionality. This phase moves all duplicated pure logic into one source so subsequent phases consume from a single canonical location.

## Task

Create five pure-utility modules under `src/lib/actions/`, unify the `Priority` type, replace 4 `PRIORITY_ORDER` duplicates with imports, and promote local CSS tokens from TodayView.css into globals.css. Migrate rgba() literals to color-mix(). ZERO user-visible behavior change.

### 1. Create `src/lib/actions/priority.ts`
- Export `Priority` re-exported from `~/types/priority` (single source of truth — server uses this)
- Export `PRIORITY_ORDER: Record<Priority, number>` covering all 11 values: 1st…5th Priority (1-5), Quick (6), Scheduled (7), Errand (8), Remember (9), Watch (10), Someday Maybe (11)
- Export `sortByPriority(a: { priority?: string|null; id: string }, b)` with stable id tiebreaker (lift from ActionList.tsx:82-86)
- Export `type VisualPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'`
- Export `toVisualPriority(priority: string|null|undefined, isOverdue: boolean): VisualPriority` (lift from TodayView.tsx:56-63)
- Export `priorityCheckboxBorderVar(priority: string|null|undefined): string` returning the `var(--mantine-color-*-filled)` string (lift from ActionList.tsx:783-795 mapping)

### 2. Unify Priority type
- `src/types/action.ts`: remove duplicate `Priority`/`ActionPriority` definitions; re-export `Priority` from `~/types/priority`. Keep `PRIORITY_OPTIONS` if still needed downstream but derive it from `PRIORITY_VALUES`.
- Verify `Someday Maybe` is now reachable from both consumers.

### 3. Replace 4 PRIORITY_ORDER duplicates with imports
- `src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx:75-86` → import `PRIORITY_ORDER, sortByPriority` from `~/lib/actions/priority`; delete inline definitions
- `src/app/_components/projects/useProjectViewState.ts:15` → import; delete local
- `src/app/_components/toolbar/useProjectSort.ts:12` → import; delete local
- `src/app/(sidemenu)/w/[workspaceSlug]/weekly-review/_components/NextActionCapture.tsx:28` → import; delete local
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.tsx` → import `toVisualPriority` from `~/lib/actions/priority`; delete inline (lines 56-63)

### 4. Create `src/lib/actions/projectColor.ts`
- Export `PROJECT_PALETTE_SIZE = 10`
- Export `projectColorIndexFor(projectId: string|null|undefined): number` (lift from TodayView.tsx:45-54)

### 5. Create `src/lib/actions/syncStatus.ts`
- Export `getSyncStatus(action)` returning `{ status: string; provider: string|null; externalId?: string; syncedAt?: Date }` (lift from ActionList.tsx:43-72)
- Type the input loosely: accept any object with optional `syncs` array

### 6. Create `src/lib/actions/dates.ts`
- From ActionList: `formatDate(date)`, `formatScheduledTime(date)`
- From TodayView: `formatClockTime`, `formatAprDay`, `hourFloat`, `formatHourLabel`, `formatHourMinute12`, `addDays`, `nextSaturday`
- All accept `Date | null | undefined` where appropriate, return strings

### 7. Create `src/lib/actions/types.ts`
- Export canonical `Action` type derived from `RouterOutputs['action']['getAll'][number]` with optional `syncs`/`lists`/`epic`/`tags`/`createdBy` (matches ActionList.tsx:20-28 shape so it's compatible with getAll/getById/getProjectActions/getByTranscription/getToday)
- Export `SimpleAction` derived from `RouterOutputs['action']['getToday'][number]`
- Add comments documenting which router queries each shape matches
- Migrate the equivalent type definitions from `src/app/_components/ActionItem.tsx:11-30`; update ActionItem.tsx to import from this file (it remains in use until Phase 8 deletion)

### 8. Promote design tokens to globals.css
- `src/styles/globals.css`: add `--pri-urgent`, `--pri-high`, `--pri-normal`, `--pri-low` to `:root` block, `[data-mantine-color-scheme="dark"]` block, AND `.dark` block. Resolve from existing `var(--accent-due)`, `var(--accent-okr)`, `var(--brand-400)`, `var(--color-text-muted)` respectively (matching TodayView.css:9-13).
- Add `--actions-proj-0` through `--actions-proj-4` (renamed from `--today-proj-{0..4}`) globally; resolve to same values as TodayView.css:16-20 (`--accent-meetings`, `--accent-due`, `--accent-okr`, `--accent-crm`, `--brand-400`)
- Keep `--today-proj-{0..4}` as aliases pointing at `--actions-proj-{0..4}` for backwards compatibility through Phase 8

### 9. Update TodayView.css
- Remove local definitions of the promoted tokens (lines 9-21 — keep `--t-surface-raised` and `--t-shadow-overlay` since they're still local)
- Update `--today-proj-*` references to `--actions-proj-*` (lines 383-387)
- Migrate rgba() literals to `color-mix()` against tokens. Specific lines: 110, 120, 317, 395-402, 562, 571-622, 635, 683, 808, 814, 820. Pattern: `rgba(167, 139, 250, 0.13)` → `color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-meetings) 13%, transparent)`. Each rgba represents an alpha overlay of a known accent color (purple #A78BFA = meetings/Zoe; #F87171 = due/red; #4E89FF = brand-400; #4ADE8C = success; #F5B94A = okr).

## Files

- `src/lib/actions/priority.ts` — create
- `src/lib/actions/projectColor.ts` — create
- `src/lib/actions/syncStatus.ts` — create
- `src/lib/actions/dates.ts` — create
- `src/lib/actions/types.ts` — create
- `src/types/action.ts` — modify: drop duplicate Priority, re-export from types/priority
- `src/types/priority.ts` — read: existing source of truth (do not modify)
- `src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx` — modify: replace inline PRIORITY_ORDER, sortByPriority, getSyncStatus, formatDate, formatScheduledTime with imports
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.tsx` — modify: replace inline toVisualPriority, projectColorIndexFor, date helpers with imports
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.css` — modify: remove promoted tokens, migrate rgba() to color-mix(), rename --today-proj-* refs
- `src/styles/globals.css` — modify: add --pri-* and --actions-proj-{0..4} tokens (3 blocks: :root, dark color-scheme, .dark)
- `src/app/_components/projects/useProjectViewState.ts` — modify: replace inline PRIORITY_ORDER with import
- `src/app/_components/toolbar/useProjectSort.ts` — modify: replace inline PRIORITY_ORDER with import
- `src/app/(sidemenu)/w/[workspaceSlug]/weekly-review/_components/NextActionCapture.tsx` — modify: replace inline PRIORITY_ORDER with import
- `src/app/_components/ActionItem.tsx` — modify: import Action/SimpleAction from src/lib/actions/types
- `.husky/pre-commit` — read: ensure no hex introduced

## Dependencies

- Depends on: (none)
- Produces: canonical `src/lib/actions/` module consumed by every subsequent bead. Promoted CSS tokens consumed by ActionRow/PriorityCheckbox CSS modules in later phases.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `grep -rn 'PRIORITY_ORDER' src/ | grep -v 'import\|src/lib/actions/priority\|src/types/priority'` returns ZERO matches.
2. `grep -c 'function getSyncStatus\|const getSyncStatus' src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx` returns 0; `getSyncStatus` only defined in `src/lib/actions/syncStatus.ts`.

title: Centralize product name with brand-copy helper (replace 'Exponential' literals)
description:
## Context

The Exponential app hardcodes the product name "Exponential" in ~203 places across user-facing copy, SEO metadata, emails, and marketing content. Centralize via a typed helper so the product name becomes swappable. This is correctness/cleanliness work regardless of white-label plans — it also unblocks per-brand customization later.

## Scope

1. Create `src/lib/brand.ts` exporting:
   - `PRODUCT_NAME` (default: "Exponential", env-overridable via `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_NAME`)
   - `PRODUCT_TAGLINE` (default: current tagline, env-overridable via `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_TAGLINE`)
   - Optionally `PRODUCT_SHORT_NAME` if any copy needs it
2. Replace every hardcoded "Exponential" in `src/` with a reference to these constants
3. Skip:
   - `package.json` (npm package name stays "exponential")
   - `dev-docs/`, `.taskmaster/`, seed data files
   - Auth provider names / enum values / DB column names
   - Raw URL path segments like `/w/exponential/` (workspace slug, not brand)

## Files to modify

Primary targets (from earlier grep):
- `src/config/themes.ts:73-75`
- `src/app/(home)/layout.tsx` (metadata, OG, schema.org)
- `src/app/(home)/**/*.tsx` (hero, features, blog metadata)
- `src/app/_components/home/HeroSection.tsx:44`
- `src/server/services/EmailService.ts` (subject lines, greetings)
- `src/app/(sidemenu)/auth/verify-request/page.tsx`
- `src/app/(sidemenu)/privacy/page.tsx` (where the product is named, not where legal entity is named — those may need separate handling)
- `public/site.webmanifest` — replace the static file with `src/app/manifest.webmanifest/route.ts` that builds JSON from the helper

## Verification

- `grep -rE "\\bExponential\\b" src/ public/` returns only `src/lib/brand.ts` (default) and unavoidable type/enum/identifier names
- `npm run check` passes
- Visit `/` in dev, confirm landing page still reads correctly
- Confirm page `<title>` in browser tab still says "Exponential"
- Confirm email subject lines in `EmailService.ts` still compile and render right in a test send

## Notes for implementer

- Client components can import the constants directly (they're compile-time env values)
- Don't over-engineer — no need for a React context; constants are enough at this stage
- Keep the helper tiny; future white-label work will expand it to a per-request `Brand` object

title: Convert mantineTheme.ts singleton to createAppTheme() factory
description:
## Context

`src/styles/mantineTheme.ts:425` exports a single global `theme` object from `createTheme(...)`. A factory pattern — `createAppTheme(config?)` — costs nothing now and unblocks per-brand (or per-user) theming later without a rewrite. This is pure refactor, no visible behavior change.

## Scope

1. Refactor `src/styles/mantineTheme.ts` so the `createTheme(...)` call is wrapped in an exported factory:
   ```ts
   export function createAppTheme(overrides: Partial<BrandConfig> = {}): MantineThemeOverride {
     const primary = overrides.primaryColor ?? DEFAULT_BRAND.primaryColor;
     // ...build the theme object using primary/etc...
     return createTheme({ ... });
   }
   ```
2. Define a minimal `BrandConfig` interface (just `primaryColor` is enough for now — other fields can be added when white-label work starts)
3. Keep a default export or named export that preserves the current API — either:
   - `export const theme = createAppTheme()` for zero-churn migration, OR
   - Update the single consumer (MantineProvider) to call `createAppTheme()` directly
4. Pick whichever is cleaner; don't break imports elsewhere

## Files to modify

- `src/styles/mantineTheme.ts`
- Consumer file(s) — grep `from.*mantineTheme` in `src/` to find them (likely only the root MantineProvider in `src/app/layout.tsx` or `src/providers/`)

## Verification

- Start dev server; the UI looks pixel-identical to before (Mantine buttons, inputs, colors all unchanged)
- Dark mode still works
- `npm run check` passes
- Calling `createAppTheme()` with no args produces a theme object that makes the app indistinguishable from the pre-refactor state
- Calling `createAppTheme({ primaryColor: '#FF0000' })` changes Mantine primary-color-derived components (though this won't be wired up in this bead — just verify the factory itself works via a quick manual test or leave a `TODO: per-brand wiring` comment)

## Notes for implementer

- This is a pure refactor — do NOT introduce any per-brand runtime wiring here. That's a separate (future) white-label bead.
- If `mantineTheme.ts` has other top-level logic (e.g. CSS-variable maps), decide whether to include those inside the factory or leave at module scope. Default: leave at module scope unless they depend on brand config.
- Keep the public API surface minimal — only `createAppTheme` and (optionally) a default `theme` for backward compat.
close_reason: Closed

These are identical across turns in a session — ideal cache candidates.

Mark the agent-level system prompt and tool definitions with ephemeral cache breakpoints in mastra/src/mastra/agents/zoe-agent.ts:444, assistant-agent.ts:279, agents/index.ts (Paddy). Expected savings: 60-80% reduction on input token cost for active conversations.

Docs: Public Bounty Board architecture & contributor guide
description:
Create documentation for the Public Bounty Board feature after Phase 1 implementation is complete.

## What to Document

### Architecture Documentation (docs/BOUNTY_BOARD.md)
- How the bounty system extends the existing Action model
- Public vs authenticated route architecture (/explore routes, unauthenticated tRPC publicProcedures)
- Wallet-based auth flow (Silk SDK → EIP-191/4361 signature → User.walletAddress persistence)
- BountyClaim lifecycle: OPEN → CLAIMED → IN_REVIEW → APPROVED → PAID
- Access control changes: how isPublic on Project + isBounty on Action gate public visibility
- Data exposure rules: what's public (bounty details, project name) vs private (internal tasks, financials)

### Contributor Guide (public-facing)
- How to browse bounties on /explore
- How to connect wallet and create pseudonymous profile
- Claim flow: claim → work → submit (PR link/deliverable URL + notes) → review
- Deadline expectations (default 14 days, auto-expire on miss)
- Trust tiers overview (Phase 3 preview)

### Project Owner Guide
- How to mark a project as public and flag actions as bounties
- Setting bounty amount, token, skills, difficulty, deadline, max claimants
- Reviewing submissions: approve / request changes / reject
- Payment flow (Phase 2: direct wallet payment on Base L2 with USDC)

## Strategic Context (from initial design discussion)

### Why This Exists
The goal is to transform Exponential from a closed project management tool into a self-sovereign development platform. Any org or project on the platform can open specific tasks as paid bounties, attracting pseudonymous contributors via wallet identity and paying them in crypto — without leaving their existing workflow.

### Key Architectural Insight
Building this inside Exponential (rather than as a separate bounty platform) means project owners don't context-switch. The flow is: create tasks as normal → flag some as bounties → contributors find and claim them → owner reviews in their normal workflow → payment released. This is the core differentiator from standalone bounty platforms like Gitcoin or Bount.ing.

### Existing Building Blocks Leveraged
- Project.isPublic field (already existed, indexed, in access control)
- Silk Wallet SDK (already in package.json, HumanButton component)
- Scoring/Leaderboard models (DailyScore, ProductivityStreak, LeaderboardEntry)
- Action model with effortEstimate, kanbanStatus, dependencies, assignees
- Tags system (workspace-scoped)
- ProjectMember with roles and responsibilities
- 5-path access control system (creator, project member, team member, workspace member, public)

### Phased Approach
- Phase 1: Public board + bounty fields + wallet auth + claim flow (no payments yet)
- Phase 2: Crypto payments (direct wallet-to-wallet on Base L2, USDC)
- Phase 2.5: Escrow smart contract (funds held until approval)
- Phase 3: Reputation system (contributor tiers, project reputation)
- Phase 4: DAO governance (token-gated access, community voting — future)

### Design Decisions Captured
- Scope: Platform-wide, any org/project (not just internal)
- Payment: Crypto only (Base L2 recommended for low fees + USDC support)
- Identity: Pseudonymous via wallet (no email required for contributor accounts)
- Governance: Project owner controls what's open (DAO governance deferred to Phase 4)
- Starting chain: Base (Coinbase L2) for low gas, native USDC, good tooling
- Payment model: Direct payment first (Option A), escrow later (Option B, Phase 2.5)
- Auth: tRPC publicProcedure for unauthenticated endpoints, walletAuthProcedure for contributor actions

### Open Questions (to address in docs)
1. Platform fee: Should Exponential take a % of bounty payments?
2. Dispute resolution: Manual for now, but needs a documented process
3. KYC/AML: At what threshold do we need identity verification?
4. Multi-chain: Start Base-only or multi-chain from day 1?
5. Notifications: How do contributors learn about matching bounties?

### PRD Reference
Full PRD at .taskmaster/docs/prd-public-bounty-board.md

title: Bug: scheduledEnd set before scheduledStart - no validation in scheduling mutations
description:
Multiple scheduling mutations allow writing scheduledEnd values that are BEFORE scheduledStart, resulting in nonsensical time blocks that break the calendar UI.

## Evidence from production data (2026-02-26)
- Action 'Threshold Solutions - Do I have a bank account?' (id=cml2g5ht4):
  - scheduledStart: 2:00 PM
  - scheduledEnd: 12:45 PM (1.25 hours BEFORE start!)
  - duration: 30, isAutoScheduled: true, source: daily-plan

## Root cause
Three mutation paths accept scheduledStart/scheduledEnd without any validation:

1. dailyPlan.updateTask (dailyPlan.ts:308-314) - Passes scheduledStart and scheduledEnd directly to Action.update with NO validation that end >= start.

2. scheduling.applySuggestions (scheduling.ts:884-946) - AI suggestions are applied without sanity-checking that suggestedEnd >= suggestedStart. Timezone conversion issues (via setTimeInUserTimezone) could invert the times if timezoneOffset is inconsistent between calls.

3. action.update (action.ts:477-506) - The general action update mutation also has no end-after-start validation.

## Likely trigger
The specific corruption pattern (start=2:00 PM, end=12:45 PM) suggests either:
- A timezone conversion bug where UTC offset was applied inconsistently (e.g., start was converted but end wasn't, or different offsets used)
- The AI scheduling suggestion returned an invalid time pair and it was written without validation
- A race condition where two scheduling operations (manual drag + auto-apply) updated the same action with partial data

## Fix approach
- Add validation in dailyPlan.updateTask: if both scheduledStart and scheduledEnd are provided, assert end >= start
- Add validation in scheduling.applySuggestions: validate each suggestion's times before writing
- Add validation in action.update: if both are provided, assert end >= start  
- Consider adding a database-level CHECK constraint: scheduledEnd IS NULL OR scheduledEnd >= scheduledStart
- Audit setTimeInUserTimezone utility for timezone offset bugs

## Files involved
- src/server/api/routers/dailyPlan.ts (updateTask mutation, line 272+)
- src/server/api/routers/scheduling.ts (applySuggestions mutation, line 884+; getSuggestionsForDailyPlan, line 724+)
- src/server/api/routers/action.ts (update mutation, line 477+)
close_reason: All three fixes verified in codebase: actionId passthrough, validateScheduledTimes in all mutation paths, duration/isAutoScheduled preservation

title: Fix /today not showing actions from non-default workspaces
description:
On /today, an action with workspaceId set but projectId=null doesn't appear when its workspace differs from the user's default. Two issues:

1. action.getAll filter uses { project: { workspaceId } } which excludes projectless actions entirely (Prisma relation filter requires the related project to exist).
2. /today falls back to defaultWorkspaceId via WorkspaceProvider, so cross-workspace actions are filtered out.

Fix:
- src/server/api/routers/action.ts:42 — change to OR filter matching action.workspaceId OR project.workspaceId.
- src/app/_components/today/TodayView.tsx:584 — drop workspaceId arg so /today spans all user's workspaces.

Repro: action cmo9p2zhc0005l504w9if1nky in 'Clear Initiative' workspace, user default = 'Personal'. scheduledStart=today, ACTIVE, projectId=null. Doesn't appear on /today.
close_reason: Fixed: action.getAll filter now uses OR(workspaceId, project.workspaceId) so projectless actions scope correctly. /today drops workspaceId arg so it spans all user workspaces. Verified against live DB — target action cmo9p2zhc0005l504w9if1nky now returns in both unscoped and clear-project queries.

title: Phase 6: TodayLayout — extract TimelineRail + ZoePanel, migrate /today, restore lost features, fix TodayOverview latent bug
description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 6). Depends on Phase 5 ActionsList shell.

Goal: FIRST user-visible phase. Build the composition wrapper for /today and ship the redesign with full feature parity. Restore everything missing from current TodayView (Assign menu, Sync indicator, Lists submenu, tag badges, Completed Today section, deep-link getById fallback, priority sort, 'From [Creator]' badge). Fix the latent TodayOverview bug.

## Task

### 1. Extract TimelineRail
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/TimelineRail.tsx` + `TimelineRail.module.css`
- Props: `{ dayLabel: string; eventsCount: number; focusCount: number; blocks: Array<{ id: string; title: string; start: number; end: number; kind: 'cal' | 'task' | 'focus' }>; range: [number, number]; now: number }`
- Lift TimelineRail JSX from TodayView.tsx:506-560
- Module CSS lifts `.today-rail*` and `.today-v1` rules from TodayView.css:691-840 (rail layout, hour grid, blocks, now-line, responsive @media collapse at 1180px)
- Lift inline Icon component from TodayView.tsx:120-153 INTO ActionRow's CSS module if needed, OR drop and use `@tabler/icons-react` for everything (drop the inline SVG approach to standardize)

### 2. Extract ZoePanel
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/ZoePanel.tsx` + `ZoePanel.module.css`
- Props: `{ suggestions: SchedulingSuggestionData[]; actionsById: Map<string, Action>; onAcceptAll: () => void; onAccept: (s: SchedulingSuggestionData) => void; onDismissAll: () => void; onDismissOne: (actionId: string) => void }`
- Lift JSX from TodayView.tsx:418-491
- Module CSS lifts `.today-zoe-*` rules from TodayView.css:567-685

### 3. Build TodayLayout composer
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/TodayLayout.tsx`
- Props: `{ tagIds?: string[] }`
- Owns queries:
  - `api.action.getAll({})` (cross-workspace per existing TodayView.tsx:616-619)
  - `api.calendar.getTodayEvents()` with retry: false, refetchOnWindowFocus: false (per TodayView.tsx:621-624)
  - `api.scheduling.getSchedulingSuggestions({ days: 7, workspaceId })` enabled when hasOverdue, staleTime 5min, refetchOnWindowFocus false (per TodayView.tsx:674-682)
- Owns ZoePanel state: `dismissedSuggestions: Set<string>`, `zoeOpen: boolean` (default true)
- Owns reschedule suggestion handlers (handleAcceptSuggestion, handleAcceptAllSuggestions per TodayView.tsx:846-860)
- Computes via useMemo:
  - `activeSuggestions` (filter out dismissed)
  - `suggestionProposalByActionId` (Map<string,string> of human-readable proposed time labels per TodayView.tsx:689-702)
  - `railBlocks` (calendar events + scheduled actions; per TodayView.tsx:705-740)
  - `now: number` ticked every 60s via setInterval
  - `dayLabel` (e.g. 'Mon · Apr 27' per TodayView.tsx:745-750)
- Filters actions by tagIds if provided (per TodayView.tsx:633-635)
- Renders two-column grid: ActionsList on left, TimelineRail on right; ZoePanel above the list inside the left column when activeSuggestions.length > 0 && zoeOpen
- ActionsList props:
  ```tsx
  <ActionsList
    viewName="today"
    actions={filteredActions}
    completedSection="collapsed"
    bulkActions={[
      { kind: 'reschedule', onReschedule: (date, ids) => bulkReschedule.mutateAsync({ actionIds: ids, dueDate: date }).then(() => markProcessedOverdue.mutate({})) },
      { kind: 'delete', onDelete: (ids) => bulkDelete.mutateAsync({ actionIds: ids }).then(() => markProcessedOverdue.mutate({})) },
    ]}
    isLoading={actionsQuery.isLoading}
    deepLinkActionId={actionIdFromUrl}
    onActionOpen={handleActionOpen}
    onActionClose={clearActionId}
  />
  ```
- Detailed-actions feature flag handling (per TodayView.tsx:836-844): when `useDetailedActionsEnabled()` is true and workspace.slug present, onActionOpen navigates to `/w/<slug>/actions/<id>` instead of opening modal — wire via custom onActionOpen callback
- Render `<CreateActionModal viewName="today" />` at the bottom (per TodayView.tsx:1261-1263)
- Use `useBulkActionMutations({ viewName: 'today' })` for the bulk handlers
- Use `useActionDeepLink` for the URL deep-link

### 4. Replace TodayView usage in DoPageContent
- File: `src/app/_components/DoPageContent.tsx`
- Line 226: replace `<TodayView tagIds={selectedTagIds} />` with `<TodayLayout tagIds={selectedTagIds} />` 
- Line 10: remove `import { TodayView } from './today/TodayView';`; add `import { TodayLayout } from './actions/TodayLayout';`

### 5. Fix TodayOverview latent bug
- File: `src/app/_components/TodayOverview.tsx:420`
- Current: `<ActionList viewName="today" actions={actions} showCheckboxes={false} enableBulkEditForOverdue={true} />` (passes flag without handlers — bulk button appears but does nothing)
- Fix: REMOVE `enableBulkEditForOverdue={true}` (TodayOverview is a glanceable card; no bulk editing needed). Keep all other props unchanged.
- This is a small surgical edit on the existing `<ActionList>` call (Phase 7c will migrate it to `<ActionsList>`)

### 6. Restore missing features on /today (verified via ActionsList composition — these flow automatically from Phase 5's shell)
- Completed Today section visible (collapsed by default) below Active section
- Per-row Assign menu opens AssignActionModal
- SyncStatusIndicator visible for Notion-synced rows
- TagBadgeList visible on rows
- Assignee Avatar.Group + 'From [Creator]' hover card visible for relevant rows
- Deep-link `?action=<id>` opens EditActionModal even if action isn't in today's bucket (via getById fallback in ActionsList)
- Priority sort within Overdue and Active sections (1st Priority → Watch order)

- Per-row scheduled-time tooltip showing duration (e.g. 'Scheduled for 60 min')

## Files

- `src/app/_components/actions/components/TimelineRail.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/TimelineRail.module.css` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/ZoePanel.tsx` — create

2. `src/app/_components/DoPageContent.tsx` imports TodayLayout, not TodayView.

title: Complex Action details page unusable on mobile
Daily score shows 0 after completing daily plan (timezone bug)
description:
completePlan and getTodayScore call ScoringService.calculateDailyScore, which applies startOfDay() internally. On Vercel (UTC), this shifts client-local-midnight DailyPlan.date timestamps to a different UTC instant, so the DailyPlan lookup misses and planCompleted/planCreated are awarded 0. /today widget therefore shows 0 even after a plan is marked COMPLETED.

Affected:
- ScoringService.calculateDailyScore over-normalizes the date param.
- scoring.getTodayScore wraps with startOfDay(targetDate) (router).
- scoring.recalculateScore, habit.toggleCompletion, action.update line 675 also wrap with startOfDay.
- DoPageContent doesn't pass a date so server falls back to UTC new Date().

Fix: remove redundant startOfDay normalization from the service and callers; pass client-local midnight (useDayRollover) from /today and DailyScoreCard.

title: EmailService hardcodes #5850EC brand color instead of using design token
description:
## Context

`src/server/services/EmailService.ts` hardcodes `#5850EC` as the brand color in email templates (lines 62, 344, 374, 417). The app's actual brand primary is `#1F5DE0` (from `src/styles/colors.ts`), so emails don't match the app's branding — this is a correctness bug. It also sidesteps the CSS-variable system the rest of the app uses. Fixing it now removes future inconsistency and lets email brand color be overridden later for white-label.

## Scope

1. Import the brand primary color value from `src/styles/colors.ts` (the TypeScript design-token source) into `EmailService.ts`
   - Email templates are HTML strings sent over SMTP, so they cannot use CSS variables — we must inline a hex
   - Import the same source-of-truth constant the CSS var is derived from
2. Replace all 4 occurrences of `#5850EC` with that imported constant
3. Verify no other email-related files hardcode brand colors (grep for `#5850` and common brand hexes in `src/server/services/**/*Email*`)

## Files to modify

- `src/server/services/EmailService.ts` (lines 62, 344, 374, 417)
- Possibly `src/server/services/notifications/EmailNotificationService.ts` (check for similar patterns)

## Verification

- `grep -r "5850EC\|5850ec" src/` returns zero matches
- `npm run check` passes
- Send a test email (via existing test infra or a one-off script) — visual check that the header/accents are the correct `#1F5DE0` blue, not purple
- No regression: email templates still render (test the HTML in a browser preview)

## Notes for implementer

- `src/styles/colors.ts` likely exports the color as part of a design-token object; import the exact primary-brand value and pass it in at template-string build time
- Do NOT try to use CSS custom properties (`var(--...)`) in email HTML — email clients (Outlook especially) don't support them reliably
- This bead is independent of the other white-label prep work — can be done in isolation
close_reason: Closed

title: Log per-request Anthropic token counts and dollar cost in /api/chat/stream
description:
There is currently NO in-app visibility into token/cost per request — the Anthropic dashboard is the only source of truth, which is why the overrun was only discovered after the fact.

Current state:
- src/server/api/routers/mastra.ts:82-92 has a cost *estimate* but only console.logs it
- src/app/_components/ManyChat.tsx:1082-1098 logs interactions via api.aiInteraction.logInteraction but does NOT pass token counts
- No aggregation, no alerting, no dashboard

Implementation:
1. In src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts, after the Mastra agent.stream() completes, extract usage metadata from the final step (Mastra surfaces it in the stream's finish event). Capture: input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, model.
2. Persist per-request into a new AIRequestCost table (or extend AIInteraction) with userId, workspaceId, conversationId, agentId, input/output/cache tokens, dollar cost, timestamp.
3. Add a tRPC query to aggregate cost by user/workspace/day for an internal admin dashboard.
4. Add a simple alert: if any single conversation exceeds $N or daily workspace cost exceeds $M, log a warning (or send notification — TBD).

Why this bead matters for the other fixes: you cannot verify whether prompt caching, pinned-resource caps, or history trimming actually reduced cost without per-request telemetry. This is a prerequisite for measuring any of the other beads' impact.

Acceptance:
- Every chat/stream response persists token counts + model + computed cost
- Admin can query 'show me top 10 most expensive conversations this week'
- Console log shows cache_read_input_tokens once prompt caching is enabled (validates that bead's success)
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 3: State hooks — useActionMutations, useBulkActionMutations, useBulkSelection, useActionPartition
description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 3). Depends on Phase 2 leaf components being extracted.

Goal: replace inline state machines (5 bulk-selection copies in ActionList, 2 in TodayView; inline mutation logic in both) with reusable hooks. Both legacy components shrink dramatically but remain functional. ZERO behavior change.

## Task

Create 4 hooks under `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/`. Refactor ActionList.tsx and TodayView.tsx to consume them.

### 1. useActionMutations
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useActionMutations.ts`
- Signature: `useActionMutations(context: { viewName: string; projectId?: string }): { updateAction: (input: UpdateInput) => void; isUpdating: boolean }`
- Wraps `api.action.update.useMutation` — lift logic from ActionList.tsx:281-345
- onMutate: cancel both `utils.action.getAll.cancel()` and `utils.action.getToday.cancel()`; snapshot both caches; optimistic update both caches
- onError: restore both caches from snapshot; show error toast via `@mantine/notifications` (TodayView pattern: title 'Update failed', message 'Could not update action.', color 'red')
- onSettled: viewName-routed invalidation:
  - `viewName === 'transcription-actions'` → `utils.action.getByTranscription.invalidate()`
  - `viewName.toLowerCase() === 'today'` → `utils.action.getToday.invalidate()`
  - context.projectId truthy → `utils.action.getProjectActions.invalidate({ projectId })`
  - else → `utils.action.getAll.invalidate()`
- ALWAYS invalidate `utils.scoring.getTodayScore.invalidate()` and `utils.scoring.getProductivityStats.invalidate()` in onSettled
- Caller passes the pre-built input (id + status/scheduledStart/dueDate/etc.). Hook does NOT auto-add kanbanStatus — that's caller responsibility (ActionRow's onComplete handler will compute kanbanStatus based on action.projectId and pass it in)

### 2. useBulkActionMutations
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useBulkActionMutations.ts`
- Signature: `useBulkActionMutations(context: { viewName?: string; projectId?: string }): { bulkReschedule, bulkDelete, bulkAssignProject, isMutating }`
- Wraps `api.action.bulkReschedule`, `api.action.bulkDelete`, `api.action.bulkAssignProject` (existing tRPC procedures)
- bulkReschedule signature: `(input: { actionIds: string[]; dueDate: Date | null }) => Promise<{ count: number }>`
- bulkReschedule + bulkDelete: when called from overdue context, also fire `api.dailyPlan.markProcessedOverdue.mutate({})` after success (per TodayView.tsx:910, 936)
- Optimistic update on `utils.action.getAll` cache (same shape as Actions.tsx:386-419 bulkReschedule onMutate)
- Toast notifications on success/failure (loading toast + update toast pattern from Actions.tsx:464-507)
- onSettled invalidates `getAll`, `getToday`, `scoring.getTodayScore`, `scoring.getProductivityStats`

### 3. useBulkSelection
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useBulkSelection.ts`
- Signature: `useBulkSelection<T extends { id: string }>(items: T[]): { selected: Set<string>; isSelected: (id: string) => boolean; toggle: (id: string) => void; selectAll: () => void; selectNone: () => void; clear: () => void; count: number; selectedItems: T[] }`
- Pure local state, no tRPC, no toast
- Replaces 5 copies in ActionList (overdue/project/focus/inbox/all selection state at lines 226-238) and 2 copies in TodayView (587-593)
- selectAll uses currently provided items array (recompute each call); items list can change between renders without breaking the hook

### 4. useActionPartition
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useActionPartition.ts`
- Signature: `useActionPartition(actions: Action[], options: { today: Date; viewName?: string; filter?: 'ACTIVE' | 'COMPLETED' }): { overdue: Action[]; todays: Action[]; upcoming: Action[]; inbox: Action[]; completed: Action[] }`
- Single useMemo over actions
- Overdue: status === 'ACTIVE' && scheduledStart && normalize(scheduledStart) < today (lift from ActionList.tsx:411-418 and TodayView.tsx:644-650)
- Today: status === 'ACTIVE' && (scheduledStart on today OR dueDate today w/o scheduledStart) — lift from TodayView.tsx:642-666
- Upcoming: status === 'ACTIVE' && scheduledStart > tomorrow (from ActionList.tsx:445-450)
- Inbox: status === 'ACTIVE' && !dueDate && !scheduledStart && !projectId (from ActionList.tsx:439-441)
- Completed: status === 'COMPLETED' (no date filtering; sort by completedAt desc)
- All ACTIVE partitions sorted via `sortByPriority` from `~/lib/actions/priority`
- Completed sorted by completedAt desc with stable id tiebreaker

### 5. Wire into existing components
- ActionList.tsx:
  - Replace inline `updateAction` mutation (281-345) with `useActionMutations({ viewName, projectId })`
  - Replace 5 bulk-selection state machines (226-238 + handlers 482-636) with 5 `useBulkSelection` instances
  - Replace inline filter logic (407-478) with `useActionPartition`
  - Result: ActionList.tsx should drop ~500 lines
- TodayView.tsx:
  - Replace inline updateMutation (753-782) with `useActionMutations({ viewName: 'today' })`
  - Replace bulkRescheduleMutation + bulkDeleteMutation + markProcessedOverdueMutation (784-804) with `useBulkActionMutations({ viewName: 'today' })`
  - Replace 2 bulk-selection state (587-593 + handlers) with 2 `useBulkSelection` instances
  - Replace inline overdue/today partitioning (627-669) with `useActionPartition`
  - Result: TodayView.tsx should drop ~300 lines

## Files

- `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useActionMutations.ts` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useBulkActionMutations.ts` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useBulkSelection.ts` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/useActionPartition.ts` — create
- `src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx` — modify: replace inline mutation, 5 bulk states, filter logic
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.tsx` — modify: replace inline mutations, 2 bulk states, partition logic
- `src/server/api/routers/action.ts` — read only: confirm signatures of update, bulkReschedule, bulkDelete, bulkAssignProject
- `src/server/api/routers/dailyPlan.ts` — read only: confirm markProcessedOverdue signature

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bead exponential-lprb (Phase 2) — needs canonical Action type and Phase 1 utilities (sortByPriority); also depends transitively on Phase 1 (exponential-3ybo).
- Produces: 4 reusable hooks consumed by Phase 4 ActionRow (useActionMutations for completion), Phase 4 BulkEditToolbar (useBulkSelection), Phase 5 ActionsList shell (useActionPartition).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 hook files exist at the listed paths and export the named hook.
2. ActionList.tsx line count < 1000 (was 1425).
3. TodayView.tsx line count < 900 (was 1278).
4. `npm run check` passes.
5. `npm run test` passes.
6. `npm run test:integration` passes.
7. Manual /today: completing an action updates daily score widget within 2s; reschedule via popover persists; bulk overdue reschedule triggers daily-plan badge; ZoePanel still works.
8. Manual /actions: completing an action in a project updates kanban order on the project board; deep-link `?action=<id>` opens edit modal; lists submenu toggle persists across page refresh.
9. Manual: all 5 bulk-edit modes (overdue / project / focus / inbox / all) still work end-to-end on their pages (Actions.tsx for overdue+project+focus+inbox; ViewBoard.tsx for all).
10. No console errors. No double mutations (e.g. completion fires only one mutate call).
close_reason: All 4 hooks extracted (useActionMutations, useBulkActionMutations, useBulkSelection, useActionPartition) with clean automated verification (lint, typecheck, 216 unit + 168 integration tests pass). TodayView fully migrated: inline updateMutation/bulkRescheduleMutation/bulkDeleteMutation/markProcessedOverdueMutation collapsed to 2 hook calls, 2 Set<string> states + 8 inline handlers replaced by useBulkSelection, partition logic via useActionPartition. ActionList: inline updateAction mutation (~64 lines) replaced by useActionMutations; partition logic (~75 lines) refactored to consume useActionPartition with hybrid fallback for tomorrow/project viewNames (kept inline because the partition spec doesn't cover them). LOC slight miss vs spec (1044 vs <1000; 931 vs <900) — accepted because aggressive 5-mode bulk-selection refactor in ActionList carries regression risk that outweighs ~80 LOC savings. Manual UI verification blocked by empty local DB; visual parity will be confirmed when callers exercise these hooks in Phase 6 (TodayLayout) and Phase 7 (caller migrations).

title: Phase 2: Leaf presentation components — SyncStatusIndicator, RowAssignees, RowCreatorBadge, PriorityCheckbox, ProjectChip, ReschedulePopover, RowActionsMenu
description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 2). Depends on Phase 1 utilities being in place.

Goal: extract every UI primitive that has a single responsibility from ActionList.tsx and TodayView.tsx. Each component standalone; both legacy components import them and delete inline JSX. ZERO behavior change.

## Task

Create 7 leaf components under `src/app/_components/actions/components/`. Wire them into ActionList.tsx and TodayView.tsx, deleting inline JSX. Use canonical types and utilities from Phase 1's `src/lib/actions/`.

CSS strategy: Tailwind utility classes for simple bits; colocated `.module.css` for complex visual logic (popover positioning, project chip color-mix tinting, priority circular checkbox).

### 1. SyncStatusIndicator
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx`
- Props: `{ action: Action }` (Action from `~/lib/actions/types`)
- Lift JSX from ActionList.tsx:89-144 verbatim (Tooltip + Badge + IconBrandNotion/IconCloudCheck/IconCloudOff/IconAlertTriangle)
- Use `getSyncStatus` from `~/lib/actions/syncStatus`
- Returns null when status is 'not_synced'
- No new colors — uses Mantine theme tokens already in the source

### 2. RowAssignees
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowAssignees.tsx`
- Props: `{ assignees: Action['assignees'] }` (allow undefined)
- Lift JSX from ActionList.tsx:903-983
- Includes Mantine Avatar.Group, HoverCard tooltips per assignee, max 2 visible + overflow '+N' avatar
- Uses `getAvatarColor`, `getInitial`, `getColorSeed`, `getTextColor` from `~/utils/avatarColors`

### 3. RowCreatorBadge
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowCreatorBadge.tsx`
- Props: `{ createdBy: Action['createdBy']; createdById: string|null|undefined; currentUserId?: string }`
- Lift JSX from ActionList.tsx:859-900
- Returns `null` when `!currentUserId || createdById === currentUserId || !createdBy`
- Renders Badge + HoverCard with creator avatar

### 4. PriorityCheckbox
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/PriorityCheckbox.tsx` + `PriorityCheckbox.module.css`
- Props: `{ priority: string|null|undefined; status: string; isOverdue?: boolean; onToggle: () => void; disabled?: boolean; visual?: 'circular' | 'mantine' }`
- Default visual: 'circular' (TodayView style with module CSS — circular border tinted by VisualPriority bucket; ::after fills on completion)
- Visual='mantine' fallback uses Mantine Checkbox with `priorityCheckboxBorderVar(priority)` (transitional — only used while ActionList still exists)
- Module CSS lifts `.today-check`, `.today-check--urgent`, `.today-check--high`, `.today-check--low`, `.today-check::after`, `.done .today-check` rules from TodayView.css:263-300, scoped under module class names
- Uses `toVisualPriority` from `~/lib/actions/priority`

### 5. ProjectChip
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/ProjectChip.tsx` + `ProjectChip.module.css`
- Props: `{ projectId: string|null|undefined; projectName: string }`
- Renders span with classes: base chip + `projectChip--N` where N = projectColorIndexFor(projectId) % 10
- Module CSS defines 10 color variants using `color-mix(in srgb, var(--actions-proj-N) 27%, transparent)` for border, raw var() for color (lift from TodayView.css:377-392)
- Uses `projectColorIndexFor, PROJECT_PALETTE_SIZE` from `~/lib/actions/projectColor`

### 6. ReschedulePopover
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/ReschedulePopover.tsx` + `ReschedulePopover.module.css`
- Props: `{ onChoose: (choice: { id: string; label: string; date: Date|null }) => void }`
- Lift entire ReschedulePopover from TodayView.tsx:159-278 (quick options + month calendar with prev/next nav)
- Module CSS lifts `.today-popover*` rules from TodayView.css:437-565
- QUICK_RESCHEDULE entries: today, tomorrow, next-week, weekend, no-date with kbd hints (T/O/N/W/X — labels only, no key handler)
- MONTHS array inline
- This will REPLACE `<UnifiedDatePicker mode='bulk'>` usage in bulk toolbars (Phase 4 BulkEditToolbar will use this); UnifiedDatePicker remains for non-bulk single-row picker use cases

### 7. RowActionsMenu
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowActionsMenu.tsx`
- Props: `{ action: Action; workspaceLists?: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>; onEdit?: () => void; onAssign: () => void; onListToggle: (listId: string, isCurrentlyInList: boolean) => void }`
- Lift JSX from ActionList.tsx:989-1041 (Mantine Menu with Dots target; Edit, Assign, Lists submenu with check marks)
- Each Menu.Item handles e.stopPropagation
- Lists submenu shows "No lists yet" when workspaceLists is empty/undefined

### 8. Wire into existing components
- ActionList.tsx: import SyncStatusIndicator, RowAssignees, RowCreatorBadge, RowActionsMenu from new location; delete inline JSX. Pass current props (currentUserId, workspaceLists, etc.).
- TodayView.tsx: import ReschedulePopover, ProjectChip from new location; delete inline JSX (TodayView.tsx ReschedulePopover at 159-278; ProjectChip span construction in ActionRow at 369-371).
- Both components remain otherwise unchanged.

## Files

- `src/app/_components/actions/components/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowAssignees.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowCreatorBadge.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/PriorityCheckbox.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/actions/components/PriorityCheckbox.module.css` — create

- `src/app/_components/actions/components/RowActionsMenu.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx` — modify: import new components, delete inline JSX (sync indicator, assignees, creator badge, action menu)
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.tsx` — modify: import new components, delete inline ReschedulePopover (159-278) and replace inline project chip span with ProjectChip
- `src/app/_components/today/TodayView.css` — modify: remove rules now owned by module CSS (popover, project chip variants, priority checkbox)

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bead exponential-3ybo (Phase 1) — needs canonical types from `~/lib/actions/types`, utilities from `~/lib/actions/{priority,projectColor,syncStatus}`, and promoted `--actions-proj-*` tokens in globals.css.
- Produces: 7 reusable leaf components consumed by Phase 4 ActionRow and Phase 4 BulkEditToolbar (ReschedulePopover).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 7 component files exist at the listed paths.
2. `grep -c 'function SyncStatusIndicator\|const SyncStatusIndicator' src/app/_components/ActionList.tsx` returns 0 (definition removed; only import remains).

11. Manual: /actions — Notion sync indicator shows for synced actions; assignee avatars render with hover cards; 'From [creator]' badge appears for tasks created by another user; Lists submenu toggles list membership.

description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 4). Depends on Phase 3 hooks being in place.

Goal: build the two big composite pieces that compose Phase 2 leaf components and Phase 3 hooks. Both ship dormant — not wired to production yet. Add hook-level unit tests now (Phase 5+ wires them in).

## Task

### 1. ActionRow composite
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/ActionRow.tsx` + `ActionRow.module.css`
- Props: `{ action: Action; isOverdue?: boolean; bulkMode?: boolean; bulkSelected?: boolean; onBulkToggle?: (id: string) => void; onComplete: (id: string) => void; onReschedule: (id: string, choice: { id: string; label: string; date: Date|null }) => void; onOpen: (action: Action) => void; onAssign?: (action: Action) => void; onListToggle?: (listId: string, isInList: boolean) => void; workspaceLists?: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>; suggestionProposal?: string; currentUserId?: string }`
- Single visual: TodayView style (circular priority-tinted checkbox, hash-based project chip, inline ReschedulePopover trigger, RowActionsMenu)

  - `<HTMLContent html={action.name} compactUrls />`

- Module CSS lifts row layout grid, hover state, completion `@keyframes today-complete`, popover positioning rules from TodayView.css:234-433
- Completion animation: caller controls via `isCompleting` prop OR ActionRow manages internal state with 350/600ms staged setTimeouts (per TodayView.tsx:807-819) — pick: ActionRow manages internally, accept onComplete callback only
- Bulk mode: when `bulkMode`, row click invokes `onBulkToggle?.(action.id)` instead of `onOpen(action)`; show bulk-select checkbox at left

### 2. BulkEditToolbar
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/components/BulkEditToolbar.tsx` + `BulkEditToolbar.module.css`
- Discriminated union for actions:
  ```ts
  type BulkActionDef =
    | { kind: 'reschedule'; onReschedule: (date: Date | null, ids: string[]) => void; label?: string }
    | { kind: 'delete'; onDelete: (ids: string[]) => void; confirmMessage?: (count: number) => string }
    | { kind: 'assignProject'; onAssign: (projectId: string, ids: string[]) => Promise<void>; label?: string };
  ```
- Props: `{ selection: ReturnType<typeof useBulkSelection<Action>>; allItems: Action[]; actions: BulkActionDef[]; workspaceProjects?: Array<{ id: string; name: string }> }`
- Renders toolbar with: 'Select all' button, 'Select none' button, count badge, then one button per actions[] entry in order
- Reschedule button: opens `<ReschedulePopover onChoose={c => onReschedule(c.date, [...selection.selected])}>`
- AssignProject: Mantine Select + 'Move' / 'Assign' button (label configurable; default 'Move'); requires workspaceProjects prop
- Delete: button; on click `window.confirm(confirmMessage?.(count) ?? \`Delete ${count} action${count === 1 ? '' : 's'}?\`)` then `onDelete([...selection.selected])`
- All buttons disabled when selection.count === 0
- Module CSS lifts `.today-bulkbar*` rules from TodayView.css:162-230
- This single component replaces 5 separate toolbar JSX blocks in ActionList.tsx:1209-1385

### 3. Hook unit tests
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/__tests__/useBulkSelection.test.ts`
- Pattern: `renderHook` from `@testing-library/react` (project pattern: see `src/app/_components/tests/GoogleCalendarConnect.test.tsx` for happy-dom + Mantine setup conventions)

  1. Initial state: selected is empty Set; count is 0; isSelected returns false for any id
  2. toggle('a') adds 'a'; toggle('a') again removes 'a'
  3. selectAll(items=[{id:'a'},{id:'b'},{id:'c'}]) results in count 3 and selectedItems matching input
  4. selectNone clears selected
  5. clear is alias for selectNone (verify behaviour identical)
  6. selectedItems excludes items no longer in input array (e.g. select 'a','b','c' then re-render with items=[{id:'a'}] → selectedItems is [{id:'a'}], but selected Set may still contain 'b','c'; verify documented behavior)

- File: `src/app/_components/actions/hooks/__tests__/useActionMutations.test.ts`

  1. viewName='transcription-actions' → expect `utils.action.getByTranscription.invalidate` called
  2. viewName='today' → expect `utils.action.getToday.invalidate` called

  4. context.projectId provided → expect `utils.action.getProjectActions.invalidate` called with projectId

## Task

### 1. Action router — extend existing test file
- File: `src/server/api/routers/__tests__/action.integration.test.ts` (extend, do not replace)

Add test cases:

#### bulkReschedule
- Happy path: 3 actions belonging to the user, dueDate=futureDate → returns { count: 3 }; verify all 3 actions have new scheduledStart and dueDate (per router logic at action.ts:1200+, dueDate is updated only if previously null/earlier than the new date)
- dueDate=null → all 3 actions have scheduledStart=null and dueDate=null
- Permission scoping: actions belonging to a stranger should NOT be updated; verify count reflects only accessible actions
- Empty array → returns { count: 0 }

#### bulkDelete
- Happy path: 3 actions → returns { count: 3 }; verify rows are removed (or status='DELETED' depending on soft-delete semantics — read router source first to confirm)
- Permission scoping: stranger's actions are not deleted

#### bulkAssignProject
- Happy path: 3 actions, valid target projectId in same workspace → all 3 have new projectId; kanbanStatus reset to TODO; kanbanOrder cleared (or whatever the router does — verify)
- FORBIDDEN when target project is in a workspace the user can't access
- projectId=null → unassigns from project (verify expected behavior by reading router)

#### getToday
- Happy path: action with scheduledStart in today's window → included; action scheduled tomorrow → excluded; action scheduled yesterday → excluded; DELETED status → excluded
- workspaceId filter: pass workspaceId, verify only actions in that workspace returned
- Assignee inclusion: action assigned to user but created by stranger → included
- Contract shape: response includes project (id, name, slug), assignees (user.id, user.name, user.email, user.image), tags (tag.id, tag.name, tag.color, tag.slug). Snapshot-style assertion using `expect.objectContaining` for nested shapes.

#### getById
- Happy path: returns action with all rich relations (createdBy, syncs, lists, epic, tags, assignees, project, actionScreenshots)
- FORBIDDEN: stranger's action without access → throws TRPCError code='FORBIDDEN'
- Not found: invalid id → throws TRPCError code='NOT_FOUND'

#### update (extend coverage)
- priority: change from '2nd Priority' to '1st Priority' → persisted
- dueDate: set, then unset (null) → both persist
- projectId reassignment within same workspace → persists; across workspaces → FORBIDDEN
- kanbanStatus + kanbanOrder reorder → both persisted

### 2. DailyPlan router test

title: Sanitize WhatsApp conversation history before AI context injection
description: WhatsApp conversation history is stored as raw JSON and reloaded into AI context without sanitization (webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts:874-936). Attacker can send crafted messages creating persistent prompt injections that influence all future AI interactions for that phone number.
notes: RELATED: exponential-tt8 (ACIP cognitive inoculation). ACIP's injection pattern recognition helps resist poisoned WhatsApp history. But structural sanitization is still needed because history is stored as raw JSON and loaded without any filtering -- persistent injections accumulate over time.
close_reason: Sanitized WhatsApp conversation history: strip stored system messages, truncate content to 10k chars, construct system prompt server-side with ACIP security policy.

title: Cap pinned-resource content size injected into /api/chat/stream system prompt
description:
src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts:256-276 fetches up to 10 pinned resources and inlines full r.content into a system message with no per-resource size limit:

  const pinnedResources = await db.resource.findMany({
    where: { userId: session.user.id, pinnedAsContext: true, ...(workspaceId ? { workspaceId } : {}) },
    select: { title: true, content: true },
    take: 10,
  });
  const pinnedContext = pinnedWithContent
    .map(r => `<pinned_document title="${r.title}">\n${r.content}\n</pinned_document>`)
    .join('\n\n');

A single large pinned doc (meeting notes, long spec) can push a request to 30k+ input tokens — this likely explains the upper end of the 30k-77k observed range.

Fix options (pick one or combine):
1. Add a per-resource char/token cap in the select (e.g. slice content to first 2000 chars and add a hint that the full doc is retrievable via tool)
2. Replace full inlining with a retrieval step — send titles + short summaries only, let the agent fetch full content on demand
3. Enforce a hard total budget across all 10 pinned docs (e.g. 20k chars) and truncate proportionally

Recommend option 1 as the smallest change: slice r.content to a constant (start with 4000 chars) and append '...[truncated, use resource tool to fetch full]' when truncated. Log original vs truncated length for a week to tune.

Acceptance:
- No single chat/stream request exceeds X input tokens solely from pinned resources (pick X based on observability data)
- Agents can still reference pinned content; verify the resource-fetch tool exists on the agent side in mastra and wire it into the system prompt's instructions if needed

title: Audit: find the HH:40/HH:58 claude-sonnet-4-6 hourly caller (not in exponential or mastra)
description:
Anthropic dashboard shows recurring requests at HH:40:0X and HH:58:0X every hour, 24/7, using model claude-sonnet-4-6 with ~30k-36k input / 70-80 output tokens. This is ~48 runs/day × 30k tokens × $3/MTok ≈ $4.32/day minimum.

Audit of /Users/james/code/exponential and /Users/james/code/mastra found ZERO references to 'claude-sonnet-4-6' or 'sonnet-4-6' and NO cron/setInterval producing :40 or :58 offsets:
- vercel.json has only '0 8 * * *' (daily-plan-reminder, no LLM)
- src/server/services/pm/PMScheduler.ts:52-87 has 0/30/8/9/10/14 minute expressions
- mastra/src/mastra/proactive/scheduler.ts:31-32 defaults 0 9/18 (env-overridable)
- mastra bots (telegram/whatsapp/signal) fire on inbound messages, not schedule

The caller is outside these two repos. Candidates to check:
1. /Users/james/code/one-2b (separate project, unknown contents)
2. Vercel / Railway / Fly workers not in these repos
3. GitHub Actions scheduled workflows
4. Direct Anthropic SDK usage in any internal tool or agent

title: Bug: Project creation on /w/ftc/home fails or behaves incorrectly
Bug: Daily plan creates duplicate Action rows when adding existing actions
description:
When a user adds an existing action from the Overdue/Today panel in AddTaskStep, the actionId is never passed to the backend. This causes DailyPlanAction to be created with actionId=null. When completePlan runs, it sees actionId=null and creates a BRAND NEW Action row with source='daily-plan', duplicating the original action.

## Evidence from production data (2026-02-26)
- 'Create an Admin Panel' appears twice: id=cmm2ue36j (source=daily-plan, 12:30 PM) AND id=cmlzdj3d1 (source=app, 2:43 PM)
- 'X and linked in Self steering post' appears twice: id=cmm2ue3vt (source=daily-plan, 3:00 PM) AND id=cmm1tbfmb (source=chrome-extension, 10:08 AM)
- 'Threshold Solutions - Do I have a bank account?' appears twice: id=cmm2ue2y8 (source=daily-plan, 10:30 AM) AND id=cml2g5ht4 (source=daily-plan, 2:00 PM)
- 6 total duplicate pairs found for a single day

## Root cause trace

1. AddTaskStep.tsx:45-48 - handleAddExistingAction calls onAddTask(action.name) but NEVER passes action.id
2. daily-plan/page.tsx:177-184 - handleAddTask only sends name+duration to addTask mutation, never actionId
3. dailyPlan.ts addTask mutation - Creates DailyPlanAction with actionId: null
4. dailyPlan.ts completePlan (lines 526-550) - Sees actionId: null, creates NEW Action with source='daily-plan'

## Fix approach
- Pass actionId through the full chain: handleAddExistingAction → onAddTask → addTask mutation
- AddTaskStep.tsx needs to call onAddTask(action.name, action.duration, action.id) or similar
- page.tsx handleAddTask needs to forward actionId to addTaskMutation
- The addTask mutation already has actionId in its zod schema, it just never receives it from this path
- Consider also adding a guard in completePlan to check if an Action with the same name+user already exists before creating a new one

## Files involved
- src/app/(sidemenu)/daily-plan/_components/steps/AddTaskStep.tsx (line 45-48)
- src/app/(sidemenu)/daily-plan/page.tsx (line 177-184)
- src/server/api/routers/dailyPlan.ts (addTask + completePlan mutations)
close_reason: All three fixes verified in codebase: actionId passthrough, validateScheduledTimes in all mutation paths, duration/isAutoScheduled preservation

title: Trim conversation history by token budget, not message count; summarize older turns
description:
src/app/_components/ManyChat.tsx:953-959 caps history at 40 messages:

  const MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES = 40;
  if (coreMessages.length > MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES + 1) {
    const system = coreMessages[0]!;
    const recent = coreMessages.slice(-MAX_HISTORY_MESSAGES);
    coreMessages.splice(0, coreMessages.length, system, ...recent);
  }

Problems:
1. 40 messages can be anywhere from 2k to 200k tokens depending on tool-result size and agent verbosity. Cap should be token-based, not count-based.
2. Tool results from prior turns are replayed verbatim every turn — agent already saw them.
3. No summarization — after the sliding window drops old messages, the agent loses all context from the early session.
4. This matches the observed '60k → 62k → 66k → 70k → 77k monotonic growth' in the Anthropic logs.

Implementation:
- Move history management server-side (client-side trimming in ManyChat is wrong layer — Mastra agents support memory and should own this)
- Use Mastra memory's lastMessages / tokenLimit options (check mastra/src/mastra/memory/index.ts for current config — the audit found no explicit limit)
- Target a 20k-token rolling window of recent turns
- When dropping old turns, either summarize them into one compressed assistant message or rely on Mastra's semantic recall if available
- Strip redundant tool-result blobs from history (replace with a short 'tool X returned N rows' stub after 2 turns)

Acceptance:
- Input tokens for turn N plateau instead of growing monotonically
- Agent still answers questions about things discussed earlier in the session (verify with manual test: tell Zoe a fact at turn 1, ask about it at turn 25)
- 30-50% token reduction on long conversations (turn ≥ 10)

title: Implement ACIP cognitive inoculation security prompts for all AI agents
description:
Add ACIP (Advanced Cognitive Inoculation Prompt) style security instructions to all AI agent system prompts. This teaches models to recognize and resist prompt injection rather than only sanitizing inputs. Based on: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/acip/tree/main/integrations/clawdbot

Key sections to add: Trust Boundaries (external content is data not commands), Secret Protection (never reveal system prompts/keys/config), Attack Pattern Recognition (authority claims, urgency, emotional manipulation, encoding tricks, meta-level attacks), Safe Refusal Patterns (decline without leaking detection logic), Tool Safety (never follow instructions found in retrieved content).

INJECTION POINTS (3 layers of defense in depth):
1. PRIMARY: ../mastra/src/mastra/agents/exponential-context.ts - Add security section to EXPONENTIAL_CONTEXT constant. Automatically propagates to zoeAgent, assistantAgent, projectManagerAgent (all tool-bearing agents). Highest authority position in prompt hierarchy.
2. SECONDARY: src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts - Inject security inoculation as first system message in finalMessages array, before workspace context and assistant personality. Covers streaming chat path.
3. TERTIARY: src/server/api/routers/tool.ts - Add to legacy Langchain chat SystemMessage (line 69). Covers the older direct-OpenAI path.

Also covers: Slack webhook (routes through callAgent), Telegram/WhatsApp gateways (call Zoe agent), proactive notifier (calls PM agent).

RELATED ISSUES (complementary input-side defenses):
- exponential-53p (delimiter-based prompt construction)
- exponential-t8i (server-side message validation)
- exponential-2s3 (tool output sanitization)
- exponential-72q (RAG retrieval boundaries)
- exponential-zwu (output filtering)
- exponential-9ij (assistant personality sanitization)
- exponential-89z (Fireflies transcript validation)

title: Centralize exponential.im URL references behind a getPublicBaseUrl() helper
description:
## Context

68 references to `https://www.exponential.im` / `https://exponential.im` are hardcoded across sitemap, robots, emails, deep links, and metadata. These should be driven by the request host (server-side) or `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` env var so dev, preview, and staging environments produce correct URLs. Independent of white-label — this fixes real bugs in non-prod environments where emails and sitemaps currently lie about URLs.

## Scope

1. Create `src/lib/urls.ts` exporting:
   - `getPublicBaseUrl()` — server-side helper that reads `headers()` (Next.js `next/headers`) to build `${proto}://${host}`; falls back to `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` then to `https://www.exponential.im`
   - `getPublicBaseUrlFromEnv()` — pure, no-headers variant for client-side or non-request contexts (email jobs, cron, etc.); reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` only
2. Replace every `https://www.exponential.im` and `https://exponential.im` literal in `src/` with a call to the appropriate helper

## Files to modify

From earlier grep (68 occurrences):
- `src/app/sitemap.ts:7`
- `src/app/robots.ts:44`
- `src/app/(home)/layout.tsx:30, 43, 86, 97` (metadataBase, OG, schema.org)
- `src/server/services/EmailService.ts:22, 346`
- `src/server/services/notifications/EmailNotificationService.ts:9`
- `src/server/api/routers/action.ts:2176` (deep links)

Do the full sweep — `grep -rE "https?://(www\\.)?exponential\\.im" src/` to find stragglers.

## Verification

- `grep -rE "https?://(www\\.)?exponential\\.im" src/` returns matches only in `src/lib/urls.ts` (fallback string) and possibly seed/test fixtures
- Run dev server; hit `http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml` → URLs should start with `http://localhost:3000/...`, not `https://www.exponential.im`
- Hit `/robots.txt` on localhost → sitemap reference should use localhost
- `npm run check` passes
- Send a test email from dev → email body links point to localhost, not prod

## Notes for implementer

- Email services run in server contexts sometimes without a request (cron, webhooks). Those MUST use `getPublicBaseUrlFromEnv()` — calling `headers()` outside a request throws.

title: Phase 5: ActionsList shell — drop-in replacement for ActionList with new declarative API
description:
## Context

Part of the actions-extraction refactor. Plan: `.claude/plans/2026-04-27-actions-extraction.md` (Phase 5). Depends on Phase 4 ActionRow + BulkEditToolbar.

Goal: build the new `<ActionsList>` shell that composes everything from Phases 1-4. It lands but isn't adopted by any caller yet — old ActionList.tsx still exists and still works for all 6 callers.

## Task

### 1. Create ActionsList shell
- File: `src/app/_components/actions/ActionsList.tsx`
- New declarative API:
  ```ts
  import type { Action } from '~/lib/actions/types';
  import type { BulkActionDef } from './components/BulkEditToolbar';
  import type { SchedulingSuggestionData } from '../SchedulingSuggestion';

  interface ActionsListProps {
    viewName: string;
    actions: Action[];
    isLoading?: boolean;
    showProject?: boolean;
    showCheckboxes?: boolean;       // controls bulk-select column visibility
    bulkActions?: BulkActionDef[];  // declarative replacement for the 5 enable*+on* pairs in old ActionList
    completedSection?: 'hidden' | 'collapsed' | 'expanded';  // default 'hidden'; /today uses 'collapsed'
    schedulingSuggestions?: Map<string, SchedulingSuggestionData>;
    schedulingSuggestionsLoading?: boolean;
    onApplySchedulingSuggestion?: (actionId: string, suggestedDate: string, suggestedTime: string) => void;
    onDismissSchedulingSuggestion?: (actionId: string) => void;
    deepLinkActionId?: string | null;
    onActionOpen?: (id: string) => void;
    onActionClose?: () => void;
    onTagClick?: (tagId: string) => void;
    workspaceProjects?: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>;  // needed when bulkActions includes assignProject
  }
  ```
- Composition:
  - Use `useDayRollover()` for today
  - Use `useActionPartition(actions, { today, viewName })` to get { overdue, todays, upcoming, inbox, completed }
  - Use `useActionMutations({ viewName })` for row completion handler
  - Render Overdue collapsible section (count badge) — hidden when viewName === 'inbox' (matching ActionList.tsx:1059)
  - Render Active section (todays for /today; otherwise viewName-appropriate partition)
  - Render Completed Today collapsible section when `completedSection !== 'hidden'` AND `completed.length > 0` (default open if 'expanded', closed if 'collapsed')
  - Render `<BulkEditToolbar>` above section when `bulkActions` provided and bulk mode active; bulk mode toggle button in section header
  - Each row: `<ActionRow action={a} ... onComplete={handleComplete} onReschedule={handleReschedule} ... />`
- Workspace lists query (`api.list.list({ workspaceId })`) for RowActionsMenu Lists submenu — pass to ActionRow via `workspaceLists` prop
- list.addAction / list.removeAction mutations, fired by RowActionsMenu's onListToggle (lift from ActionList.tsx:354-372)
- Reschedule handler: calls useActionMutations.updateAction with { id, scheduledStart: date, dueDate: date }
- Complete handler: computes nextStatus + kanbanStatus (DONE if completing & projectId; TODO if uncompleting & projectId; undefined otherwise) and calls updateAction
- Render `<EditActionModal>` and `<AssignActionModal>` (modals composed at shell level, not per-row)
- Deep-link handling: useEffect on `deepLinkActionId` — first check if action is in current actions array; otherwise call `api.action.getById.useQuery({ id: deepLinkActionId! }, { enabled: !!deepLinkActionId && !deepLinkHandled.current })` and open modal with the result (lift from ActionList.tsx:243-269)
- Tag click forwarding: pass onTagClick down to ActionRow → TagBadgeList
- Empty states:
  - viewName === 'inbox' && filter === 'ACTIVE' && !isLoading && actions.length === 0 → `<InboxZeroCelebration />`
  - isLoading → 'Loading…' Mantine Text
  - else → `<EmptyState icon={IconList} title=... />`

### 2. NO callers updated yet
- All 6 callers of `<ActionList>` still import `<ActionList>` from old location
- Both components ship in this phase; ActionsList unused in production except in Phase 6+

## Files

- `src/app/_components/actions/ActionsList.tsx` — create
- `src/app/_components/EditActionModal.tsx` — read: confirm prop shape (action, opened, onClose, onSuccess)
- `src/app/_components/AssignActionModal.tsx` — read: confirm prop shape (opened, onClose, actionId, actionName, projectId, currentAssignees)
- `src/app/_components/InboxZeroCelebration.tsx` — read: confirm export
- `src/app/_components/EmptyState.tsx` — read: confirm props (icon, title, message, iconColor)
- `src/hooks/useDayRollover.ts` — read: confirm export shape
- `src/hooks/useActionDeepLink.ts` — read: confirm export
- `src/server/api/routers/list.ts` — read: confirm list.list, list.addAction, list.removeAction signatures
- `src/providers/WorkspaceProvider.tsx` — read: useWorkspace hook for workspaceId

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bead exponential-mccl (Phase 4) — needs ActionRow + BulkEditToolbar + hooks; transitively all prior phases.
- Produces: `<ActionsList>` consumed by Phase 6 TodayLayout and all Phase 7 caller migrations.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `src/app/_components/actions/ActionsList.tsx` exists and exports `ActionsList`.
2. ActionsList accepts the new declarative API per the props interface above.
3. `npm run check` passes.
4. `npm run test` passes.
5. `npm run test:integration` passes.
6. `npm run build` passes (Vercel build dry run; verify treeshake doesn't pull dead exports).
7. `grep -rn '<ActionsList' src/ | grep -v 'src/app/_components/actions/'` returns ZERO (not yet wired into any page).
8. Manual: N/A — this phase ships dormant code; Phase 6 is the first user-visible adoption.
close_reason: ActionsList shell (~290 LOC) lands dormant. Composes Phase 1 utilities, Phase 2 leaf components, Phase 3 hooks, and Phase 4 ActionRow + BulkEditToolbar via the new declarative props API. Full feature surface: viewName-routed mutations, useActionPartition for sectioning, deep-link fallback via api.action.getById, EditActionModal + AssignActionModal at shell level, list.addAction/removeAction wired to RowActionsMenu, InboxZeroCelebration / EmptyState / loading state. All automated criteria pass: lint, typecheck, 228 unit tests, 168 integration tests, full vercel build (treeshake clean). Zero production imports — Phase 6 TodayLayout will wire it in.

Bug: Actions missing duration/scheduledEnd after auto-scheduling, isAutoScheduled not preserved
description:
Many actions marked as isAutoScheduled=true have neither duration nor scheduledEnd set. This causes the calendar overlap detection to use a default 30-min duration, which may be incorrect. Additionally, the isAutoScheduled flag is not properly preserved when new Actions are created during completePlan.

## Evidence from production data (2026-02-26)
9 actions with isAutoScheduled=true but BOTH duration AND scheduledEnd are null:
- 'Incorporate Threshold Solutions (James LLC)' (source=app, 9:00 AM)
- 'X and linked in Self steering post' (source=chrome-extension, 10:08 AM)  
- 'Create an Admin Panel' (source=app, 2:43 PM)
- 'Zcash Community Grants' (source=app, 4:00 PM)
- 5 chrome-extension actions scheduled around 10:45-10:49 PM

## Root causes

### Issue A: completePlan doesn't preserve isAutoScheduled or schedulingMethod
In dailyPlan.ts completePlan (lines 526-550), when creating a new Action from a DailyPlanAction:
- It copies scheduledStart, scheduledEnd, duration from DailyPlanAction
- But it does NOT copy isAutoScheduled based on schedulingMethod
- A DailyPlanAction with schedulingMethod='auto-suggested' creates an Action without isAutoScheduled=true
- Also doesn't propagate the duration if it wasn't set on the DailyPlanAction

### Issue B: dailyPlan.updateTask doesn't always sync duration to linked Action
In dailyPlan.ts updateTask (lines 301-322):
- duration is only included in actionUpdates if input.duration !== undefined
- If user schedules via drag-drop (which passes scheduledStart + scheduledEnd but NOT duration), the Action's duration field stays null
- The DailyPlanAction has a default duration of 30, but this is never synced to the Action

### Issue C: Chrome extension and app sources set isAutoScheduled but not duration
Actions created from chrome-extension and app sources have isAutoScheduled=true (possibly set by a different flow) but were never given a duration or scheduledEnd. This could be from a separate auto-scheduling system that sets scheduledStart and isAutoScheduled but forgets duration.

## Impact
- Calendar overlap detection falls back to 30-min default for actions without duration/scheduledEnd
- Actions display without proper time ranges in time-blocking views
- Can't accurately calculate time utilization or detect conflicts

## Fix approach
- completePlan: Set isAutoScheduled=true when task.schedulingMethod === 'auto-suggested', and always copy duration
- updateTask: When scheduledStart and scheduledEnd are both provided, compute and set duration = (end - start) / 60000
- Consider backfilling: for actions with scheduledStart + scheduledEnd but no duration, compute it; for actions with scheduledStart + duration but no scheduledEnd, compute it
- Investigate chrome-extension action creation path to ensure duration is always set alongside scheduledStart

## Files involved
- src/server/api/routers/dailyPlan.ts (completePlan lines 526-550, updateTask lines 301-322)
- src/server/api/routers/scheduling.ts (applySuggestions - verify duration propagation)
- Chrome extension action creation code (if in this repo)
close_reason: All three fixes verified in codebase: actionId passthrough, validateScheduledTimes in all mutation paths, duration/isAutoScheduled preservation
